---
title: "PilotStudy"
format: html
---

# Libraries
```{r}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false

library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
```

# Reading and Cleaning Data
```{r}
data <- read_csv("pilot.csv")

data$CookingMethod <- factor(data$CookingMethod)
data$DryingMethod  <- factor(data$DryingMethod)
data$Replicate <- factor(data$Replicate)

data <- data %>%
  group_by(Replicate, DryingMethod, CookingMethod) %>%
  summarise(NumQuarters = mean(NumQuarters), .groups = "drop")
```

# Fitting a Split-Plot Model
```{r}
fit <- lmer(NumQuarters ~ CookingMethod * DryingMethod +
              (1 | Replicate:DryingMethod),
            data = data)

summary(fit)
anova(fit)
#------------------------------------------------------------
set.seed(403)

beta_0  <- 4.75
beta_B  <- 0.25
beta_A  <- 24.75
beta_AB <- -9.25

sigma_wp <- 1.118
sigma_sp <- 1.976

alpha <- 0.05
nsim <- 1000

simulate_power <- function(nrep) {
  
  sig_count <- 0
  
  for (s in 1:nsim) {
    
    sim_data <- expand.grid(
      Replicate = factor(1:nrep),
      DryingMethod = c("Air", "Oven"),
      CookingMethod = c("Microwave", "Stove")
    )
    
    wp <- unique(sim_data[, c("Replicate", "DryingMethod")])
    wp$u <- rnorm(nrow(wp), mean = 0, sd = sigma_wp)
    
    sim_data <- merge(sim_data, wp, by = c("Replicate", "DryingMethod"))
    
    X <- model.matrix(~ CookingMethod * DryingMethod, data = sim_data)
    mu <- as.vector(X %*% c(beta_0, beta_B, beta_A, beta_AB))
    
    sim_data$NumQuarters <- mu + sim_data$u + rnorm(nrow(sim_data), mean = 0, sd = sigma_sp)
    
    fit_sim <- tryCatch(
      suppressWarnings(
        lmer(NumQuarters ~ CookingMethod * DryingMethod +
               (1 | Replicate:DryingMethod),
             data = sim_data,
             REML = FALSE)
      ),
      error = function(e) NULL
    )
    
    if (!is.null(fit_sim)) {
      pval <- tryCatch(
        suppressWarnings(anova(fit_sim)["CookingMethod:DryingMethod", "Pr(>F)"]),
        error = function(e) NA
      )
      
      if (!is.na(pval) && pval < alpha) {
        sig_count <- sig_count + 1
      }
    }
  }
  
  sig_count / nsim
}

rep_range <- 2:6
power_vals <- sapply(rep_range, simulate_power)

data.frame(
  Replicates = rep_range,
  Power = power_vals
)

```
